original: The Roman Senate and Praetorian Guard proclaim 13-year-old Gordian III as Emperor - the youngest-ever emperor of a united Rome
Emperor Julianus of Constantinople's "School Edict" prohibit Christian teachers from using pagan literature, which was standard text for classical education at the time
Avars under Khagan Bayan begin the siege of Constantinople, which fails, preserving the Eastern Roman Empire
Byzantine city Thessalonica is sacked by Saracen pirates led by renegade Leo of Tripoli
Battle of Strumitsa Valley: Byzantines destroy Bulgarian armies
Battle of Stiklestad: Norwegian King Olaf II is killed while trying to reclaim the throne, one of the most famous Norwegian battles
Lando Sittino proclaims (anti-)pope Innocent III
Emperor Go-Horikawa, aged only 10 years old, ascends to the Chrysanthemum Throne of Japan
Turkish fleet recaptures Djerba from Spaniards
Great Britain executes Agnes Waterhouse, the first British woman convicted of witchcraft in Chelmsford, England
Antwerps request union with Utrecht
University of Franeker / University of Friesland opens in the Netherlands
Duke Farnese's troops ready for the invasion of England
French colonistSamuel de Champlainsurrenders Montreal to British privateers led by David Kirke after a blockade of the colony
Dutch fleet under Johannes van Walbeeck lands on Curaçao
The largest town hall in the world at the time opens in Amsterdam
Dutch VOC troops and their allies attack the fort of Asaudi, driving out Makasar and Hoamoalese defenders during the Great Ambon War (modern Indonesia)
Nathaniel Bacon is declared a rebel for assembling frontiersmen to protect settlers from Native Americans
War of the Grand Alliance: Battle of Landen/Neerwinden - France wins a Pyrrhic victory over Allied forces in the Spanish Netherlands
Ten Spanish treasure galleons sink off Florida's coast in a hurricane
First international world title prizefight: Jack Stack of England defeats challenger M. Petit of France in 29 minutes in England
First schoolhouse west of the Allegheny Mountains is completed in Schoenbrunn, Ohio

Santa Marta earthquakes destroy much of the capital city of Santiago de los Caballeros de Guatemala, New Spain (now Antigua, Guatemala); over 500 people are killed instantly, and 600 more die later from resulting disease and starvation
Skaftár Volcano in Iceland erupts, killing about 9,000 people
The Pittsburgh Gazette, the first newspaper west of the Alleghenies, is published
John Graves Simcoe decides to build a fort and settlement at Toronto after sailing into the bay
African Episcopal Church of St. Thomas in Philadelphia is dedicated
First sugar plantation in Hawaii is established
Inauguration of the Arc de Triomphe in Paris
Cumberland School of Law is founded in Lebanon, Tennessee, USA. At the end of 1847, only 15 law schools exist in the United States.
Irish Potato Famine: Tipperary Revolt - an unsuccessful nationalist revolt against British rule is put down by police
Annibale de Gasparis discovers asteroid 15 Eunomia
Treaty of Amity and Commerce, also known as the Harris Treaty, is signed between the US and Japan, opening Japanese ports to trade
American Civil War: Confederate spy Belle Boyd is arrested by Union troops and detained at the Old Capitol Prison in Washington, D.C.
American Civil War: Battle of Macon, Georgia (Stoneman's Raid)
American Civil War: third and last day of the Battle at Deep Bottom Run, Virginia
Major Walter Clopton Wingfield patents a portable tennis court
First motorcycle race at Manhattan Beach, New York
Southern California Golf Association forms
The First Hague Convention concerning land war customs and laws is signed
King Umberto I of Italy is assassinated in Italy by Italian-born anarchist Gaetano Bresci
Union of Orthodox Rabbis of US and Canada forms
First helicopter ascent in Douai, France
St. Louis Browns' future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcher Rube Waddell strikes out 16 Philadelphia A's in a 5-4 win against his previous team at Sportsman's Park II, St. Louis
J.W.E.L. Hilgers is the first Dutchman to fly above Dutch territory
Boston Red Sox's Joe Wood no-hits St. Louis Browns, 5-0
Independence of the Principality of Albania recognized by the Conference of London
Austria-Hungary bombs Belgrade
British fleet leaves Portland and passes through the Strait of Dover
First transcontinental phone link made between New York City and San Francisco
In response to Austria's declaration of war on Serbia, Russian diplomats and generals urge general mobilization, but the Tsar calls for partial mobilization

Cochrane and Matheson forest fire, 500,000 acres (2,000 km²) in size in Ontario, takes approximately 223 lives and leads to improved techniques and legislation for the prevention and control of forest fires
Construction of the Link River Dam begins as part of the Klamath Reclamation Project, Oregon
First successful transcontinental US airmail flight from New York to San Francisco
New language rules give equal rights to the Flemish and Walloons in Belgium
Greek troops defeat Turkish forces and are on their way to Constantinople, but the Allies forbid them from taking the city
The Communist Party of Germany (KPD) holds Struggle Day against fascism in Germany
First iron lung installed at Bellevue Hospital in New York
Phil Mead scores his 100th century, Hampshire vs. Northants
Cleveland Indians score 17 in the first two innings to defeat the New York Yankees 24-6 at Dunn Field, setting a record with 24 singles in one game
115°F (46°C) is recorded in Holly Springs, Mississippi (state record)
Airship R100 makes the first passenger-carrying flight from England to Canada
Great Depression: in Washington, D.C., U.S. troops disperse the last of the "Bonus Army" of World War I veterans
Japanese troops occupy Peking and Tianjin
Tongzhou Incident: East Hopei Army mutinies against Japanese troops and civilians
Olympic National Park is established in Washington state, featuring the longest undeveloped coastline in America
SV Urk Dutch soccer team forms
Eastern Blvd in the Bronx, NYC, is renamed Bruckner Boulevard in honor of former Bronx Borough President and US Congressman Henry Bruckner
Transport #12 departs Drancy, France, with 1001 French Jews sent to Auschwitz concentration camp; five survive until the war's end
Nazis evacuate Hollandsche Schouwburg in Amsterdam
One million inhabitants flee Hamburg
Allied air forces bomb Germany for 6 hours
MLB Cincinnati Reds' Frank McCormick hits a home run off New York Giants' Ace Adams in both games of a doubleheader
US fourth Armored Division occupies Avranches, Normandy
Gas leak explodes in a beauty parlor, killing 10 women in Harrisonburg, Virginia
Pee Wee Reese hits the 3,000th home run for the Dodgers
First non-stop transpacific flight by a jet
US bomber shot down by Soviet Air Defence Forces in the Sea of Japan, north of Vladivostok
Smokey Burgess hits three home runs to help Pirates defeat Reds 16-5
USSR performs nuclear test
WCKT (now WSVN) TV channel 7 in Miami, Florida (IND) begins broadcasting
International Atomic Energy Agency is formed by the United Nations
Southern Pacific Bay ferries stop operating in San Francisco
First United States Congress elections in Hawaii as a state of the Union
Philadelphia Phillies lose 4-3 to the SF Giants at Connie Mack Stadium, the first of an MLB record 23 consecutive defeats
Wallis and Futuna Islands become a French overseas territory
Beatles movie "Help!" has Royal World Premiere at the London Pavilion Theatre in the West End of London; Princess Margaret and the Earl of Snowdon attend
Major League record 26 strikeouts: Phillies (16), Pirates (10)
The USSR performs a nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Bob Dylanis hurt in a motorcycle accident near Woodstock, New York
Nigerian counter-coup: Head of State General Aguiyi Ironsi is assassinated by a group of Northern Nigerian officers and soldiers
A 6.5 earthquake strikes Caracas, Venezuela, causing significant damage
Fire aboard aircraft carrier USS Forrestal in the Gulf of Tonkin kills 134 sailors
The Doors' single "Light My Fire" hits #1 in the US
Arenal Volcano erupts in Costa Rica, killing 87 people and burying three small villages
Cincinnati Reds' George Culver no-hits Phillies 6-1
Gram Parsons refuses to play with the Byrds in South Africa
Mariner 6 begins transmitting distant-encounter photos of Mars
Six days of race riots in Hartford, Connecticut
France conducts a nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Singer-songwriter Gilbert O'Sullivan starts a five-week run at no. 1 with his single "Alone Again (Naturally)"
Greek plebiscite chooses republic over monarchy
Led Zeppelin has over $200,000 in cash stolen from a safe deposit box at the New York Hilton Hotel
Episcopal Church ordains female priests
France conducts a nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Second impeachment vote against President Nixon by the House Judiciary Committee
Ford becomes the first US President to visit Auschwitz concentration camp
In New York City, the "Son of Sam" kills one person and seriously wounds another in the first of a series of attacks
Seychelles gains independence (Independence Day)
The USSR performs an underground nuclear test
American Penny Dean swims the English Channel in a record 7 hours and 40 minutes; record holds until 1995
Anti-apartheid protesters against the Springbok rugby tour are confronted by police, who use batons to stop them from marching to the South African consulate in New Zealand
Cleveland Metroparks Zoo Amphitheater is dedicated in Cleveland, Ohio
Deposed Iranian President Abolhassan Banisadr flees to Paris after being smuggled aboard an Iranian Air Force Boeing 707, having shaved his eyebrows and mustache and dressed in a skirt
"Friday Night Videos", a late-night music program, premieres on NBC TV
American cyclists Alexi Grewal and Connie Carpenter win the road race double around the streets of Mission Viejo at the Los Angeles Olympics
19th NASA Space Shuttle mission (51-G): Challenger 8 launches
Bomb attack in West Beirut kills 30
Dennis Amiss scores his 100th century, Warwickshire vs. Lancashire
New York jury rules National Football League violated antitrust laws and awards United States Football League $1 in damages
Baltimore trades Mike Boddicker to the Red Sox for Brady Anderson and Curt Schilling
FDIC bails out First Republic Bank, Dallas, with $4 billion
Judge orders NASA to release unedited tape from Challenger cockpit
Rick Sutcliffe steals home, becoming the first pitcher since Pascual Perez in 1984 to do so
South African government bans anti-apartheid film "Cry Freedom"
Javier Sotomayor of Cuba sets a high jump record at 8.046 ft (2.45 m) in San Juan, Puerto Rico
Vince Coleman's record streak stops at 50 consecutive stolen bases
Boston Red Sox set a Major League record with 12 doubles in a game
South Africa Communist Party begins first legal conference
First Sunday Night game at Shea Stadium (Mets defeat Cubs 6-0)
American actor Ray Sharkey is arrested for narcotic possession
American Michael Ray Barrowman swims a world record in the 200 m breaststroke (2:10.16) at the World Championships in Perth, Australia, breaking his own record set in August 1991
Arkadiusz Skrzypaszek of Poland defeats Hungarian Attila Mizsér by 113 points (5,559 points) to win the pentathlon gold medal at the Barcelona Olympics; Poland also wins the team gold
Yevgeni Sadovyi of the Unified Team swims a world record in the 400 m freestyle in 3:45.00 to complete the 200-400 m freestyle double at the Barcelona Olympics
Cincinnati Reds pitcher Thomas Browning is arrested for marijuana possession
Israeli Court of Appeal overturns conviction of John Demjanjuk 5-0, stating insufficient evidence he is Concentration Camp guard Ivan the Terrible
Walter Koenig (Chekov in Star Trek) suffers a mild heart attack
200,000 Muslims demand the death of feminist writer Taslima Nasrin in Bangladesh

American minister and anti-abortionist Paul Jennings Hill murders Dr. John Britton and Britton's bodyguard, retired Air Force Lieutenant Colonel James Barrett, outside an abortion clinic in Pensacola, Florida
Former Italian Prime Minister Bettino Craxi receives an 8½-year jail sentence on corruption charges; he flees to Tunisia, never to return to Italy
Henny Eman's Aruban People's Party wins parliamentary election in Aruba
India army kills 27 Muslim militants
Jesse Timmendequas rapes and murders Megan Kanka, 7, resulting in Megan's Law
Carolina Panthers defeat Jacksonville Jaguars in their first NFL exhibition game 20-14
The controversial child protection portion of the Communications Decency Act (1996) is struck down as too broad by a U.S. federal court
Deadliest mass shooting in Georgia, USA, history; gunman targets Atlanta-area day trading firms, killing 12 and injuring 13 others
Astronomers announce their discovery of dwarf planet Eris
Temperature reaches 103°F (39.44°C) in Seattle, Washington, the hottest day on record
The Walt Disney Company announces the sale of Miramax Films to Filmyard Holdings for $660 million
Tropical Storm Khanun kills 88 people and leaves 60,000 homeless in North Korea
300 prisoners are freed after a Taliban attack in Dera Ismail Khan, Pakistan
44 people are killed in a wave of car bombings in Iraq
US Coast Guard opens new headquarters in Washington, DC, and is officially designated as the Douglas A. Munro Coast Guard Headquarters Building in honor of USCG's only WWII Medal of Honor recipient
€103 million worth of diamonds is stolen from the Carlton Intercontinental Hotel, Cannes, France
Chicago Cubs player John Baker scores in the bottom of the 16th for a 4-3 win over the Colorado Rockies at Wrigley Field in the longest game (6 hours, 27 minutes) in Cubs history
France contributes 8 million euros in aid to Gaza
One hundred Gazans are killed overnight by Israeli strikes
"Sweat" by Lynn Nottage premieres at the Oregon Shakespeare Festival (Pulitzer Prize for Drama 2017)
27 people are killed and 149 are injured after a truck runs into a religious procession in Zacatecas, Mexico
Microsoft launches Windows 10
Mullah Mohammed Omar, Taliban leader, is confirmed dead (killed in 2013) by the Afghan government
James VIis crowned King of Scots at Stirling
Spanish KingPhilip IIarrests plotters Antonio Perez and Princess van Eboli
Samuel de Champlainshoots and kills two Iroquois chiefs at Ticonderoga, New York, setting the stage for French-Iroquois conflicts for the next 150 years
Society of Independent Artists is founded in Paris by Albert Dubois-Pillet, Odilon Redon,Georges Seurat, and Paul Signac
SirRobert Baden-Powellforms the Boy Scouts in England
PirateHonus Wagner, at 41, hits a grand slam home run
Mexican rebelPancho Villasurrenders
Adolf Hitlerbecomes leader of the National Socialist German Workers Party
Cleveland's 125th anniversary celebration:Cy Young, aged 54, pitches two innings
Albert Einsteinspeaks about pacifism in Berlin
Test footage is first created forWalt Disney's "Steamboat Willie," featuring Mickey Mouse
Police shootout withBonnie ParkerandClyde Barrow's gang in Iowa leaves one member, Clyde's brother Buck Barrow, dead and one captured; Bonnie and Clyde escape
KingGeorge VIopens the XIV Summer Olympic Games at Wembley Stadium in London
Publication of "The Fellowship of the Ring," the first volume of "TheLord of the Rings" byJ. R. R. Tolkien, by George Allen & Unwin in London
Jacques Cousteau's Calypso anchors in a record 7,500 meters of water
Floyd PattersonTKO's Tommy Jackson in the 10th round for the heavyweight boxing title
Jack Paar's "The Tonight Show" premieres on NBC
US PresidentEisenhowersigns into law the National Aeronautics and Space Act of 1958, establishing NASA
Bob Dylanis not seriously injured in car accident in New York City
First show by rock trio "Cream," consisting of guitaristEric Clapton, bassist Jack Bruce, and drummer Ginger Baker, at the Twisted Wheel nightclub in Manchester, England
MLB St. Louis Cardinals'Lou Brocksteals his 700th base
Military coup by General Mohammed leads to the ousting of Nigerian PresidentYakubu Gowon
On Old Timer's Day, the NY Yankees announce thatBilly Martinwill return as the NY Yankees manager in 1980, and Bob Lemon will become the GM
David Bowiestars in the dramatic stage play "The Elephant Man" at the Auditorium Theatre in Denver, Colorado
MLB San Diego Padres first basemanSteve Garveyends his National League record of 1,207 consecutive games streak

Tina Turnerfilms the music video for the song “One Of The Living” at Eastern State Penitentiary in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania; the song is from the film "Mad Max: Beyond Thunderdome," in which she co-stars with Mel Gibson
Ben & Jerry's Ice Cream andJerry Garciaagree on a new flavor: Cherry Garcia, featuring cherry ice cream with cherries and fudge flakes
Gorbachevpushes plan to elect a Soviet president and parliament in March 1989
Philadelphia Phillies retire #32 in honor of pitcherSteve Carlton
White Sox trade Harold Baines to Rangers for Scott Fletcher andSammy Sosa
Yankee Stadium fans throw cups and blowup dolls at JoseCanseco
Monica SelesdefeatsMartina Navratilovain her return to tennis after she was stabbed in an on-court attack in 1993
MLB Los Angeles Dodgers managerTommy Lasordaretires, with a record of 1599-1439, four National League Pennants, and two World Series championships.
"The Princess Diaries," starringAnne Hathaway(in her film debut), featuringJulie Andrews, and directed byGarry Marshall, premieres at El Capitan theater in Hollywood
Marco van Bastenis named the manager of the Netherlands national soccer team
Authorities reverse ban on steel-string guitars in prison cells in England and Wales after a campaign by rock guitarists including Billy Bragg,David Gilmour, and Johnny Marr
Five emissaries dispatched byKublai Khanfrom the Mongol Yuan dynasty are beheaded in Japan
The "Invincible" Spanish Armada is sighted approaching England, and several skirmishes follow, forcing the invading fleet to make a long and costly retreat around Scotland and Ireland
